picks.rationale: The Knicks are in dominant form, holding a 3-0 series lead and riding a 6-game winning streak, averaging a +15.0 point differential in their last 10 games. They have covered the spread in 80% of their last five head-to-head matchups against the 76ers. The line movement from an opening of Knicks +1 to the current -1.5 suggests sharp money is backing New York to close out the series. While Joel Embiid is probable for the 76ers, the Knicks' strong overall performance and top-tier defense should enable them to cover this small spread against a struggling Philadelphia team.

Recent playoff games between these two teams have trended towards lower scores, with two of the last three going under the total. The Knicks possess a formidable defense, ranking 5th in the league in opponent points per game and limiting the 76ers to 94 and 102 points in their last two encounters. Philadelphia's offense has struggled, averaging only 103.5 points in their last 10 games. With the heightened intensity of a potential series-clinching Game 4, both teams are expected to prioritize defense, contributing to an under result.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Minnesota Timberwolves
picks.rationale: The Spurs are currently leading the series 2-1, including a decisive 38-point victory in Game 2 and a road win in Game 3, showcasing their strong form with a 7-3 record in their last 10 games. Victor Wembanyama's exceptional 39-point, 15-rebound, 5-block performance in Game 3 demonstrates his ability to dominate. The line has moved from an opening of Spurs -4.5 to -5.5, indicating increased confidence in San Antonio. With Donte DiVincenzo out for the Timberwolves and Anthony Edwards playing through a knee injury, the Spurs have a significant advantage.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Minnesota Timberwolves
picks.rationale: Two of the last three playoff games in this series have exceeded the total, with scores of 115-108 and 133-95. The Spurs boast a high-powered offense, ranking 3rd in the league with 119.8 points per game and averaging 116.6 points in their last 10 outings. While Minnesota has defensive capabilities, their need to push the pace at home in a crucial Game 4, combined with San Antonio's offensive efficiency, should lead to a higher-scoring contest. The upward line movement on the total from 216.5 to 218.5 also suggests an expectation of more points.

The Knicks hold a commanding 3-0 series lead and have won their last five head-to-head matchups against the 76ers, demonstrating clear superiority. Their impressive 6-game winning streak and robust defensive play, allowing only 100.6 points per game in their last 10, further solidify their position. Despite playing on the road, New York has already secured a victory in Philadelphia in this series. With the opportunity to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals, the Knicks will be highly motivated to close out the series with another win.

Montreal is coming off a dominant 5-1 victory over Buffalo in Game 2 of this playoff series, tying the series at 1-1. They hold a strong home record of 24-15-2 this season. While Buffalo has a good away record, the Canadiens' recent performance and home-ice advantage, coupled with key injuries for the Sabres (Ostlund, Kulich, Danforth are out, though Carrick is available), give them a significant edge to take the series lead.

The previous game in this series saw 6 goals (Montreal 5, Buffalo 1), easily hitting the over. Both teams have shown strong offensive capabilities this season, with Buffalo scoring 283 goals (5th in NHL) and Montreal scoring 279 goals (7th in NHL) in the regular season. With the series tied and both teams looking to gain an advantage, a high-scoring affair is likely, especially considering Montreal's recent offensive outburst.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Anaheim Ducks
picks.rationale: Vegas demonstrated strong offensive form in Game 3, scoring 6 goals against the Ducks. The Golden Knights have been prolific on the road recently, netting 16 goals in their last three away games. Conversely, Anaheim's defense at home has been vulnerable, allowing four or more goals in eight of their last 11 home games. With Mark Stone's injury potentially impacting Vegas's defense, and Mason McTavish returning for Anaheim, this game has a high probability of exceeding the 6.5 total.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Anaheim Ducks
picks.rationale: Despite Vegas leading the series 2-1 and winning Game 3 handily, the Ducks have a strong historical trend as home underdogs against Pacific Division opponents, winning 10 of their last 11 such games. Furthermore, the Golden Knights have struggled to cover the puck line as road favorites after a road win, failing to do so in each of their last 12 instances. With captain Mark Stone's uncertain status due to a lower-body injury, Anaheim has a good chance to keep this game close or even win outright.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Anaheim Ducks
picks.rationale: Mitch Marner is an active player for the Vegas Golden Knights and has been in excellent offensive form, recording a hat trick in Game 3 and accumulating nine points in his last four games. He leads the Golden Knights in playoff points with 13, including 6 goals and 7 assists. Given his recent performance and pivotal role in the Golden Knights' offense, Marner is highly likely to register at least one point in today's game against the Ducks.

The Washington Nationals are in strong recent form, boasting a 4-game winning streak and a 6-4 record in their last ten games. Conversely, the Miami Marlins are struggling, with a 2-game losing streak and a 4-6 record over their last ten outings, including losing five of their last six home games. The Nationals also secured a 4-3 victory over the Marlins just yesterday, and have covered the runline in four of their last five head-to-head matchups. This combination of current form, recent head-to-head success, and the Marlins' home struggles makes the Nationals moneyline a high-confidence pick.

The Athletics have demonstrated a clear head-to-head advantage over the Baltimore Orioles, winning four of their last five matchups and holding a 7-5 record against them in the last three seasons. Furthermore, the Athletics have consistently covered the runline in five of their last six games against the Orioles. While the Orioles are on a three-game winning streak, these victories came against the struggling Miami Marlins, which may inflate their perceived form. The Athletics' historical dominance in this matchup provides a strong edge for this moneyline pick.

The Philadelphia Phillies are in excellent form, especially at home, having won their last four home games against the Colorado Rockies and securing a 9-7 victory over them yesterday. In stark contrast, the Colorado Rockies are on a dismal six-game losing streak, indicating severe struggles. The Phillies also hold a superior historical head-to-head record against the Rockies, winning 88 games to their 68. Given the Phillies' strong home performance and the Rockies' current poor form, covering the -1.5 spread is a high-confidence play.

The Milwaukee Brewers enter this game with significant momentum, having won their last two contests against the New York Yankees, including a 6-0 shutout on May 8th and a 4-3 victory on May 9th. The Yankees' offense was notably stifled in these recent matchups, scoring only three runs combined. While the Yankees generally have a strong record, the Brewers are playing at home and have demonstrated the ability to shut down New York's lineup. This recent head-to-head success and home-field advantage make the Brewers moneyline an attractive high-confidence pick.

The Seattle Mariners possess a significant head-to-head advantage against the Chicago White Sox, holding a 4-1 record in their last five encounters. The White Sox are also severely hampered by multiple key injuries, including Austin Hays, Tanner Murray, and Everson Pereira, which significantly impacts their lineup and depth. Furthermore, the White Sox have struggled at home against the Mariners, losing four of their last five head-to-head games in Chicago. The Mariners' strong historical performance in this matchup, coupled with the White Sox's injury woes, makes them a high-confidence moneyline pick.

picks.game: Columbus Crew @ New York City FC
picks.rationale: Both New York City FC and Columbus Crew have shown defensive vulnerabilities this season, with NYCFC conceding 2.0 goals per match in their last five and Columbus conceding 1.7 goals per game in their last ten. Head-to-head history strongly supports a high-scoring affair, with 65% of their previous 26 meetings and 6 of their last 10 matchups going over 2.5 goals. Despite attacking injuries on both sides, their defensive struggles and historical trends suggest that this game is likely to see at least three goals. The market also leans towards the Over at -145, indicating confidence in a higher-scoring contest.

Minnesota United FC enters this match in significantly better form, boasting a 6-2-3 record and a 55% win rate compared to Austin FC's 3-4-4. Despite missing key midfielders Carlos Harvey, Julian Gressel, and Peter Stroud due to injury, Minnesota has demonstrated strong overall performance this season. Austin FC, while having no reported injuries, has shown inconsistent form, especially on the road. Playing at home, Minnesota United should leverage their superior season-long performance and tactical approach to secure a victory against a struggling Austin side.

LAFC boasts a strong home record with four wins, one draw, and one loss this season, and a very good overall form of 6-3-2. While they are coming off a 0-4 loss in the Concacaf Champions Cup, this home MLS fixture provides an opportunity to rebound. Houston Dynamo, despite recent good form across all competitions, has a poor away record this season and is missing key defenders Sam Vines, Jimmy Maurer, and Lucas Halter due to injury. LAFC has won their last two head-to-head matches against Houston by a 2-0 scoreline, giving them a significant edge at home.

The head-to-head history between LAFC and Houston Dynamo strongly suggests a low-scoring affair, with the last four meetings producing only five combined goals. LAFC's goalkeeper, Hugo Lloris, leads Major League Soccer with eight clean sheets this season, indicating a robust defense. While Houston has shown some attacking prowess, their poor away form and significant defensive injuries to Sam Vines, Jimmy Maurer, and Lucas Halter could lead to a more cautious approach or further defensive struggles. Given these trends and LAFC's defensive strength, betting the Under 2.5 at favorable odds of +115 presents a strong value.

Denis Bouanga is a prolific goalscorer for LAFC, having tallied 5 goals in 11 appearances this season. He formed a sensational partnership with Son Heung-Min, who leads MLS in assists, providing ample scoring opportunities. LAFC is playing at home against a Houston Dynamo side that has key defensive injuries to Sam Vines, Jimmy Maurer, and Lucas Halter. Given LAFC's strong home form and Bouanga's consistent scoring threat, he is well-positioned to find the back of the net in this matchup.
